summary: Scorching China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider into jazzy second‑set jam
blurb: The show shines on the second set with a fiery China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, a monster Truckin' that blossoms into Eyes of the World, and Keith Godchaux’s hot ’73 keyboards. The first half is solid but the audience tape is muddy, making the latter SBD portion the highlight.
review: On March 30, 1973 at Rochester’s Community War Memorial Auditorium the Dead delivered a two‑set marathon that still feels fresh. After a respectable first set of classics, the second set erupts with a scorching “China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider” pairing that launches into “Jack Straw,” then rolls into a monster “Truckin'” jam that seamlessly resolves into an inspired “Eyes Of The World.” Keith’s piano work is repeatedly praised as “the hottest ’73 keys,” adding bright, jazzy color to the improvisations. While the early portion of the recording suffers from a muddy audience feed, the clean soundboard capture from “Playing In The Band” onward makes the second half an essential listen for any Deadhead looking for that 1973 magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is clearly positive (highlighting China Rider, Truckin' and hot keyboards), another mixed (noting first‑set sound issues) and a third negative (no performance detail). The net sentiment leans slightly positive because the positive remarks are specific and consistent across multiple songs, outweighing the limited criticism.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard feed from the second half captures the hot keyboards and jam highlights; first‑half audience tape is muddy but the latter portion is download‑worthy.

key_highlights: Scorching “China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider” segue that launches a jazzy second set
"Truckin'" jam flows into an inspired "Eyes Of The World"
Keith Godchaux’s keyboards described as the hottest ’73 keys
Second‑half SBD quality is clear, making the performance stand out despite first‑half mud

band_performance.Brent: Keith’s piano work is repeatedly praised as the hottest ’73 keyboards, adding bright, jazzy color to the jams.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
